title: Our 2026 Grammy Award Predictions - RANGE
text:
Song of the Year Prediction

Will Win: "luther" by Kendrick Lamar & SZA

Should Win: "Abracadabra" by Lady Gaga

Should Have Been Here: "What Was That" by Lorde

Analysis: The category spans from "carefully authored intimacy to collaborative gravitas," with "luther" positioned as the Academy's most comfortable choice due to being "culturally legible in a way that flatters its own self-image."

In contrast, "Abracadabra" is described as the nominee that "resists easy interpretation altogether, treating pop as volatile, excessive, and thrillingly unconcerned with being understood."

The critic notes the conspicuous absence of Lorde's "What Was That," arguing this demonstrates how "the Grammys remain uneasy with songs that capture a real feeling before culture has decided what to do with them." This pattern suggests institutional hesitation toward emotionally raw material that defies immediate cultural categorization.
